champion.narrative: No champion exists. Cristian Carluccio (Head of Procurement & Property, Decision Maker Contact Role, SF 29 Jun) is the primary contact, but there is zero evidence he is advocating for Zycus internally, has access to the Economic Buyer (CFO or C-suite), or is driving the deal forward. The 16 Mar Next_Step__c says he is 'building support internally' and 'requested reconnect late March' (SF), which is now 103 days overdue with no follow-up call (no Avoma record). This suggests Cristian was engaged in March but has either deprioritized the project, hit internal resistance, or is waiting on Zycus to deliver the promised slides, cost estimates, and demo—none of which Zycus has provided (no completed tasks, no Avoma handoff). A true champion would be pulling Zycus into meetings, introducing the Economic Buyer, sharing internal evaluation timelines, and pushing back on competing vendors or status quo inertia. Cristian has done none of this; he made a polite request in March and has been silent since. The Contact Role of 'Decision Maker' is a Salesforce label, not evidence of championship. Aman Nand (Procurement Category Manager, Contact Role, SF 29 Jun) is even less engaged—no tasks, emails, or calls reference him beyond initial contact role assignment. No other stakeholder (finance, IT, operations, executive sponsor) is mapped or engaged (SF OpportunityContactRole 29 Jun; Avoma zero calls). The deal is single-threaded to a procurement contact who is passive, not a champion. The move is to re-engage Cristian with a direct ask: 'We committed to slides, cost estimates, and a demo in March but never followed up. Can we schedule a call this week to deliver those and discuss your internal timeline? Also, who else should we bring into the conversation (finance, IT, your manager) to ensure this moves forward?' If Cristian does not respond or deflects, escalate to executive connect (deal owner's manager Carl Kimball) to explore whether the deal is real or should be closed lost.

competition.narrative: No competitor intelligence exists. Salesforce Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are null (SF Q3 read 29 Jun). Zero Avoma calls means no buyer has mentioned evaluating other vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer, GEP) or running an RFP. The Salesforce fields How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c and Existing_vendor__c are null (SF Q3 read 29 Jun), so we do not know whether I-MED currently uses a procurement platform (Coupa incumbent is plausible given market penetration in ANZ healthcare) or is operating on manual/ERP-native processes. The Next_Step__c (16 Mar) and Next_Step_History__c contain no mentions of competing vendors (SF Q2 read 29 Jun). No tasks or emails in the 90-day scan reference competitive positioning, RFP responses, or vendor shortlist discussions. The lack of competition data has two possible reads: (1) I-MED is sole-sourcing to Zycus (unlikely given $200k spend and healthcare procurement governance norms), or (2) Zycus has zero visibility into the buyer's evaluation process and does not know who else is in the mix. The second is far more likely and far more dangerous—if I-MED is running a silent RFP or has engaged Coupa/Ariba for a renewal/upsell, Zycus is flying blind and cannot position differentiation, influence criteria, or counter competitive claims. The move is to ask Cristian directly on the discovery call: Are you evaluating other procurement platforms? Are you in contract with a current vendor (Coupa, Ariba, other), and if so, when does that contract expire? What are your concerns with your current solution (if any), and what would make you switch? If Cristian names a competitor, immediately run a competitive positioning workshop with the deal team and product marketing to map the wedge (Merlin AI negotiation agent, supplier risk, P2P workflow automation) and how to activate it in the next demo.

identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are all null (SF Q3 read 29 Jun). Zero Avoma discovery calls means no I-MED stakeholder has described dissatisfaction with their current procurement process, cost inefficiency, manual invoice handling, supplier onboarding bottlenecks, lack of spend visibility, or any other pain Zycus P2P + Merlin AI would solve. The Next_Step__c (16 Mar) mentions Cristian is 'building support internally,' but gives no clue what problem he is solving or what pain he is using to justify the business case. The Salesforce field How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c is null, and the Existing_vendor__c / Replacing_What__c fields are null (SF Q3 read 29 Jun), so we do not even know whether I-MED currently uses Coupa, SAP Ariba, Oracle, or a manual/ERP-native process. Without confirmed pain, there is no validated reason for I-MED to change vendors, and the default outcome is status quo / do nothing. This is the most critical MEDDPICC gap after Economic Buyer: pain is the engine that drives urgency, budget approval, and vendor selection. The move is to run a pain-discovery call with Cristian and Aman, asking: What is not working in your current procurement process? What are the top 3 pain points your team faces day-to-day (invoice exceptions, supplier onboarding delays, lack of spend analytics, manual PO approvals)? What is the cost (time, money, risk) of continuing with your current process? Who in the organization feels this pain most acutely (AP team, procurement ops, finance)?

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and implementation scoping are entirely unaddressed. Salesforce has no paper-process fields populated, and zero Avoma calls means no discussion of MSA, SOW, data privacy requirements (GDPR/healthcare data sovereignty for Australia), security questionnaires, or implementation timeline. The Next_Step__c (16 Mar) mentions 'Provide high-level implementation and service cost estimates,' which Zycus has not delivered (no completed follow-up task, no Avoma handoff call), so even a rough implementation scope (duration, resource needs, ERP integration lift, Coupa migration plan, Merlin AI onboarding) is missing. For a multi-module P2P deployment (iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake) in a large healthcare network, implementation will likely be 6-9 months with ERP integration, supplier onboarding, and user training; contracting will require healthcare data compliance (Australian Privacy Principles, potentially HIPAA-equivalent for patient-linked procurement data), InfoSec review, and legal redlining. None of this has been scoped or discussed. The Current_Contract_Expiration__c field is null (SF Q2 read 29 Jun), so there is no incumbent contract forcing function to anchor the timeline. This gap is not immediately blocking (paper process comes later in the cycle), but the lack of even preliminary implementation scoping is a red flag—it suggests the buyer has not thought through what replacing their current system actually entails, which increases do-nothing risk. The move is to include implementation timeline, cost, and ERP integration requirements in the discovery call, and to ask whether I-MED has begun internal planning for a P2P system replacement (project team, budget approval, IT resource allocation).
